verdict_reasoning: TGOLN2 (TGN46/TGN48/TGN51) is a type I integral membrane protein with a cytoplasmic tail and luminal/extracellular domain that primarily resides in the trans-Golgi network, cycling between TGN and early endosomes. However, it is well-documented in the literature that a fraction of TGN46 reaches the plasma membrane during its recycling itinerary before being retrieved back to the TGN. This constitutive minority surface pool — the protein cycles through the PM as part of its TGN-endosome trafficking loop — qualifies as dual localization. The extracellular domain faces outward at the PM during this transit, making it accessible to surface-directed binders in that window.
